title: fraxinus albicans
intro: Fraxinus albicans, also known as white ash, is a deciduous tree native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and Mexico. The bark and leaves of the Fraxinus albicans plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Fraxinus albicans include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and providing relief from pain and swelling. Fraxinus albicans can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its preparations and uses should be consulted with a healthcare professional before consumption.
